channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Indonesian UN peacekeeper dies from injuries sustained in Lebanon attack
item.description: The United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) announced say one Indonesian soldier, Corporal Rico Pramudia, don die for hospital after e suffer serious injuries on March 29 during attack wey ha...

item.title: 'Dancing With the Stars' Season 35 Reveals First Reality TV Contestants
item.description: ABC's 'Dancing With the Stars' is set to return for its 35th season this fall, introducing two new reality television stars as the first confirmed contestants. Maura Higgins, known for her participati...

item.title: Government raises petrol and diesel prices by over Rs26 amid weekly revisions
item.description: The Government of Pakistan has increased the prices of petrol and high-speed diesel (HSD) by Rs26.77, according to an official announcement issued by the Petroleum Division on April 24, 2026. Followin...

item.title: Global Trend Shows Significant Increase in Mobile Phone Restrictions in Schools
item.description: Over the past three years, there has been a notable global shift in how schools manage mobile phone usage. According to the Unesco Global Education Monitoring Report 2026, the proportion of education ...

item.title: The Impact of Long COVID on Children: One Teen's Experience
item.description: Long COVID is a chronic condition affecting children months after a COVID-19 infection, with symptoms varying widely by age. Sixteen-year-old Kaylee Joaquim from South Shore illustrates the challenges...

item.title: Raghav Chadha announces joining BJP, claims large-scale defection from AAP in Rajya Sabha
item.description: Raghav Chadha, a prominent leader of the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP), has announced his decision to join the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP). Chadha, in a recent statement, also revealed that nearly two-thirds...

item.title: President Ruto Highlights Development Projects and Investments in Central Kenya
item.description: President William Ruto has reaffirmed his commitment to advancing development initiatives in Central Kenya, dismissing recent criticism as baseless. During a tour in Murang'a County, he emphasised his...

item.title: Incomplete Lists Delay Fuel Subsidy Distribution for Taxi Drivers
item.description: Several taxi drivers seeking temporary relief from high fuel prices were not included in the initial government lists for the P5,000 fuel subsidy. According to a report by '24 Oras,' the Land Transpor...

item.title: NBI files trafficking complaint against Mike Defensor's wife and family
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) has filed a trafficking complaint against former congressman Mike Defensor's wife, Julie, and their child, among others. The complaint, based on the Anti-Tra...

item.title: Glasgow Influencer Jailed for Attempting to Smuggle £150,000 Worth of Cannabis into the UK
item.description: A 23-year-old social media influencer from Glasgow, Ellie Crampsie, has been sentenced to 16 months in prison for attempting to smuggle 17 kilos of cannabis, valued between £115,000 and £151,000, into...

item.title: The Role of Publishing and Copyright in Preserving South African Knowledge and Language
item.description: On World Book and Copyright Day, Dr Phindile Dlamini reflects on the vital role books play in South Africa as instruments of cultural preservation, intellectual development, and social transformation....

item.title: Study Evaluates How Major Chatbots Respond to Users Exhibiting Delusional Behavior
item.description: A recent preprint study by researchers from City University of New York and King’s College London explored how leading AI chatbots respond to users exhibiting symptoms associated with schizophrenia-sp...

item.title: Public Trust and Accountability Take Centre Stage Ahead of Freedom Day
item.description: As South Africa marks Freedom Day on 27 April and reflects on 30 years since the adoption of its Constitution, concerns about public trust in government are taking centre stage. Dr Marianne Camerer fr...

item.title: Investigation launched after 31 sloths die in storage facility ahead of planned Orlando attraction
item.description: Florida wildlife authorities are investigating the deaths of 31 sloths that were intended for a new tourist attraction in Orlando, named Sloth World. The sloths, sourced from Peru and Guyana, perished...

item.title: Dan Walker withdraws allegations in tribunal case
item.description: Dan Walker, the former presenter of Channel 5's '5 News', has withdrawn his allegations in a tribunal case. The case centred around claims of mistreatment and harassment during his time working at the...

item.title: Citizen Arrested for Social Media Post Comparing Tarnol Railway Crossing to Strait of Hormuz
item.description: In a peculiar incident, a citizen named Khurram was taken into custody by the Islamabad Police for mocking the Tarnol Railway Crossing by comparing it to the Strait of Hormuz, a key shipping route aff...

item.title: New Archive Tool Prompts Families to Confront Possible Nazi Pasts
item.description: A newly launched online database by German newspaper Die Zeit is making it far easier for people to investigate whether their relatives were members of the Nazi Party. By digitizing records from the U...

item.title: DVP ramp closure causing gridlock to end earlier than expected
item.description: A months-long closure of a ramp on the Don Valley Parkway (DVP) in Toronto, which has contributed to major traffic gridlock, is now set to end ahead of schedule. The closure, part of a long-term const...

item.title: Kysaiah Pickett shines as Melbourne Demons overcome Richmond Tigers
item.description: Melbourne Demons’ Kysaiah Pickett showcased an outstanding all-around performance in their 54-point win over Richmond. Spending part of the week in Darwin for his daughter's birthday, Pickett returned...

item.title: Keir Starmer Faces Growing Controversy Over Appointment of Peter Mandelson Amid Epstein Links
item.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer's political career is under intense scrutiny following revelations of his close ties to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. In September 2025, Starmer appointed Pete...
